post.description: Login user and retrieve JWT token
Logout user and remove JWT token
Get information about the current user

202.description: Verification email sent if account exists

400.description: Bad request, invalid token or password

get.description: Verify email address using token (from email link)

post.description: List capabilities for the current requester
Reset (hard reboot) virtual machine instance(s). Users can reset their own instances; providers can reset instances on their nodes; admins can reset any instance.

403.description: Forbidden - not authorized to reset this instance

post.description: Start instance(s). Starts all VMs and containers associated with the instance. Users can start their own instances; providers can start instances on their nodes; admins can start any instance.

403.description: Forbidden - not authorized to start this instance

post.description: Stop (gracefully shutdown) instance(s). Stops all VMs and containers associated with the instance. Users can stop their own instances; providers can stop instances on their nodes; admins can stop any instance.

403.description: Forbidden - not authorized to stop this instance

post.description: Terminate instance(s). Users can terminate their own instances; providers can terminate instances on their nodes; admins can terminate any instance.

403.description: Forbidden - not authorized to terminate this instance

post.description: Add a new network with multiple IP ranges
List networks with full details including IP ranges and executors
Cancel an active drain - brings nodes back to normal operation
Start draining nodes - prevents new instances and schedules termination after grace period

403.description: Forbidden - not authorized to manage this node

post.description: Force drain - immediately terminates all instances (only when nodes are already draining)

400.description: Node is not in draining state

post.description: Resume nodes - bring them back online after being drained

400.description: Node is not in drained state

post.description: Create a reservation for a running instance and attach it

400.description: Invalid request format or instance variant ID not found

post.description: Extend validity of the active reservation bound to an executor

409.description: Charge for provided request ID already exists
Charge for provided request ID already exists

post.description: List teams the authenticated user is a member of

get.description: Get details of a specific team including its members

parameters.name: team_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the team to retrieve

put.description: Add a new member to a team by their email

parameters.name: team_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the team to add the member to

requestBody.description: Email of the user to add to the team

post.description: Remove a member from a team by their email

put.description: Update the role of a team member

command.description: Docker command to run the recipe

image.description: Docker image corresponding to the recipe

ports.description: Ports to expose in the format <host>:<container>:<tcp|udp|sctp>

description.example: VM images with various Linux distributions.

metadata.description: JSON string with metadata for the recipe group

tags.description: Tags for the recipe group to help users find it and filter by them

description.example: Docker image with Jupyter, PyTorch and CUDA installed for easy machine learning.

details.description: Recipe object with the recipe details

global.description:
If true, create a global recipe (no owner). Only admins can set this to true.
Regular users' recipes are always owned by them.

group_name.description: Optional group name to add the recipe to

instructions_url.description: URL to the instructions for the recipe, if available

logo_url.description: Logo of the recipe, if available, e.g. Ubuntu, PyTorch or Jupyter logo

tags.description: Tags for the recipe to help users find it and filter by them

team_id.description:
Optional team ID to assign ownership to a team instead of the user.
The user must be a member of the team to create team-owned recipes.

 once it is received from the server it should be sent back in the next request
